subject: Project Management
title: IT's Estimation Problem
description: A quick win: understand IT's Estimation Problem.

hook.text: The Standish Group found that only 29% of IT projects succeed. The rest are late, over budget, or cancelled.

buildup.text: Software is uniquely hard to estimate: invisible, complex, and constantly changing during development.

discovery.text: Large IT projects run 45% over budget and 7% over time while delivering 56% less value than predicted.

twist.text: The average IT project overrun hasn't improved in 25 years of measurement. Technology changes; bias doesn't.

climax.text: Software's estimation problem is the planning fallacy's most expensive and persistent expression.

punchline.text: Software: where the last 10% takes another 90%.

quiz.question: What percentage of IT projects succeed according to research?
